literal_meaning: things put together, fabrications, constructed phenomena
definition: Constructed activities or conditioned formations; in paṭiccasamuppāda contexts this project prefers choices.
notes: Project preference is intentionally context-sensitive here. In paṭiccasamuppāda, saṅkhārā is rendered as choices because the sequence points to conditioned, consequential activity rather than a flat inventory of things. In broader contexts, especially formulaic or doctrinal ones, constructed things or formations may be more accurate depending on what is being described. Ven. Dr. M. Punnaji often explains sa?kh?r? as activities or constructs in broader existential reflection; the repository records those as source-backed alternates while retaining its current context-sensitive house treatment. The OSF glossary repeatedly explains sa?kh?ra in mental-formation and conditioned-phenomena language; the repository records those as source-backed alternates while keeping its current context-sensitive treatment.

context_rules.context: In general doctrinal or descriptive contexts referring to compounded phenomena
context_rules.notes: Use when the emphasis is on what is put together rather than on intentional activity.

context_rules.context: In analytical or technical discussions of conditioned mental processes
context_rules.notes: Use where choices would sound too narrow.

context_rules.context: In Punnaji-style existential reflection on unstable conditioned process
context_rules.notes: Source-backed alternate, especially where the term points to ongoing conditioned doing rather than a static inventory.

context_rules.context: In OSF-glossary style explanatory prose about conditioned or remembered mental patterning
context_rules.notes: Source-backed alternate, not the house default.

example_phrases.notes: Here choices would not be the best rendering.
